text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step.

text: What we think, we become.
author: Buddha

text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.

text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.

text: The best way out is always through.

text: Well done is better than well said.

text: Whether you think you can or you think you can't, you're right.

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.

text: Do not wait to strike till the iron is hot, but make it hot by striking.

text: Out of clutter, find simplicity.

text: Quality is not an act, it is a habit.

text: Nothing is impossible; the word itself says 'I'm possible'.

text: You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.

text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.

text: Little by little, one travels far.

text: Everything you can imagine is real.

text: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.

text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.

text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow is our doubts of today.

text: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.

text: Not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.

text: You are never too old to set another goal or to dream a new dream.

text: Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without.

text: The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.

text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.

text: A river cuts through rock, not because of its power, but because of its persistence.

text: Every moment is a fresh beginning.
author: T.S. Eliot

text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
author: Lao Tzu

text: When you realize nothing is lacking, the whole world belongs to you.

text: To the mind that is still, the whole universe surrenders.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Silence is a source of great strength.
author: Lao Tzu

text: He who knows that enough is enough will always have enough.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Respond intelligently even to unintelligent treatment.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Do the difficult things while they are easy and do the great things while they are small.
author: Lao Tzu

text: You only lose what you cling to.

text: Drop by drop is the water pot filled.

text: Better than a thousand hollow words is one word that brings peace.
author: Buddha

text: There is no path to happiness: happiness is the path.

text: Wherever you go, go with all your heart.

text: Our greatest glory is not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.

text: Real knowledge is to know the extent of one's ignorance.

text: The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
author: Confucius

text: Everything has beauty, but not everyone sees it.
author: Confucius

text: Study the past if you would define the future.
author: Confucius

text: The obstacle is the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Confine yourself to the present.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it.

text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
author: Seneca

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.

text: Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants.

text: No man is free who is not master of himself.
author: Epictetus

text: First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do.

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.

text: Wonder is the beginning of wisdom.
author: Socrates

text: Beware the barrenness of a busy life.

text: The beginning is the most important part of the work.
author: Plato

text: Courage is knowing what not to fear.
author: Plato

text: It is during our darkest moments that we must focus to see the light.
author: Aristotle

text: Pleasure in the job puts perfection in the work.
author: Aristotle

text: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
author: Aristotle

text: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you have imagined.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: It's not what you look at that matters, it's what you see.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: This world is but a canvas to our imagination.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: An early-morning walk is a blessing for the whole day.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: Live in the sunshine, swim the sea, drink the wild air.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: The creation of a thousand forests is in one acorn.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: For every minute you are angry you lose sixty seconds of happiness.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: The earth laughs in flowers.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Once you make a decision, the universe conspires to make it happen.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Every artist was first an amateur.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: The mind, once stretched by a new idea, never returns to its original dimensions.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: If I have seen further it is by standing on the shoulders of giants.
author: Isaac Newton

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Look deep into nature, and then you will understand everything better.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Life is like riding a bicycle. To keep your balance you must keep moving.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Creativity is intelligence having fun.
author: Albert Einstein

text: I have no special talent. I am only passionately curious.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Painting is just another way of keeping a diary.

text: Every child is an artist. The problem is how to remain an artist once we grow up.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Learn the rules like a pro, so you can break them like an artist.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Action is the foundational key to all success.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Inspiration exists, but it has to find you working.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: I dream of painting and then I paint my dream.

text: Great things are done by a series of small things brought together.

text: I would rather die of passion than of boredom.
author: Vincent van Gogh

text: If you hear a voice within you say 'you cannot paint,' then by all means paint, and that voice will be silenced.

text: Normality is a paved road: it's comfortable to walk, but no flowers grow on it.

text: Have no fear of perfection — you'll never reach it.
author: Salvador Dalí

text: Art washes away from the soul the dust of everyday life.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Creativity takes courage.
author: Henri Matisse

text: There are always flowers for those who want to see them.
author: Henri Matisse

text: Learning never exhausts the mind.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Art is never finished, only abandoned.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Nature is the source of all true knowledge.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Time stays long enough for anyone who will use it.

text: In the depth of winter, I finally learned that within me there lay an invincible summer.
author: Albert Camus

text: Real generosity toward the future lies in giving all to the present.
author: Albert Camus

text: Life isn't about finding yourself. Life is about creating yourself.
author: George Bernard Shaw

text: We don't stop playing because we grow old; we grow old because we stop playing.
author: George Bernard Shaw

text: Progress is impossible without change, and those who cannot change their minds cannot change anything.
author: George Bernard Shaw

text: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Be patient toward all that is unsolved in your heart and try to love the questions themselves.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: The quieter you become, the more you can hear.
author: Ram Dass

text: Be here now.
author: Ram Dass

text: You can't stop the waves, but you can learn to surf.

text: Wherever you go, there you are.
author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

text: Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Smile, breathe and go slowly.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Drink your tea slowly and reverently, as if it is the axis on which the world earth revolves.

text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Walk as if you are kissing the earth with your feet.
author: Thich Nhat Hanh

text: Realize deeply that the present moment is all you ever have.

text: Sometimes letting things go is an act of far greater power than defending or hanging on.

text: Yesterday I was clever, so I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, so I am changing myself.

text: The wound is the place where the light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: What you seek is seeking you.
author: Rumi

text: Raise your words, not your voice. It is rain that grows flowers, not thunder.

text: Let the beauty of what you love be what you do.
author: Rumi

text: Where there is ruin, there is hope for a treasure.
author: Rumi

text: The art of knowing is knowing what to ignore.

text: Do not be satisfied with the stories that come before you. Unfold your own myth.
author: Rumi

text: The bamboo that bends is stronger than the oak that resists.

text: Fall seven times, stand up eight.
author: Japanese proverb

text: The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
author: Chinese proverb

text: Be not afraid of growing slowly, be afraid only of standing still.
author: Chinese proverb

author: English proverb

text: When the winds of change blow, some people build walls and others build windmills.
author: Chinese proverb

text: However long the night, the dawn will break.
author: African proverb

text: If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
author: African proverb

text: A journey is best measured in friends rather than miles.

text: The world is full of magic things, patiently waiting for our senses to grow sharper.
author: W.B. Yeats

text: Happiness can be found even in the darkest of times, if one only remembers to turn on the light.

text: It is our choices that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.

text: All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: Even the smallest person can change the course of the future.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: There is some good in this world, and it's worth fighting for.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: Courage doesn't always roar. Sometimes courage is the quiet voice at the end of the day saying, 'I will try again tomorrow.'

text: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.

text: Be less curious about people and more curious about ideas.

text: I never lose. I either win or learn.

text: May your choices reflect your hopes, not your fears.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Do what you can, with what you have, where you are.

text: Comparison is the thief of joy.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: Keep your face always toward the sunshine, and shadows will fall behind you.

text: Be curious, not judgmental.

text: Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: Dwell in possibility.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: Forever is composed of nows.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars.

text: Be yourself; everyone else is already taken.

text: To live is the rarest thing in the world. Most people exist, that is all.

text: With freedom, books, flowers, and the moon, who could not be happy?

text: It is never too late to be what you might have been.

text: Life shrinks or expands in proportion to one's courage.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: We don't see things as they are, we see them as we are.

text: Each moment is a place you've never been.
author: Mark Strand

text: The only person you are destined to become is the person you decide to be.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: What you do today can improve all your tomorrows.

text: Rest and self-care are so important. When you take time to replenish your spirit, it allows you to serve others from the overflow.
author: Eleanor Brownn

text: Almost everything will work again if you unplug it for a few minutes, including you.
author: Anne Lamott

text: You can't use up creativity. The more you use, the more you have.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Try to be a rainbow in someone's cloud.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Nothing will work unless you do.
author: Maya Angelou

text: If you don't like something, change it. If you can't change it, change your attitude.
author: Maya Angelou

text: We may encounter many defeats but we must not be defeated.
author: Maya Angelou

text: My mission in life is not merely to survive, but to thrive.
author: Maya Angelou

text: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
author: Maya Angelou

text: The most difficult thing is the decision to act, the rest is merely tenacity.

text: Adventure is worthwhile in itself.
author: Amelia Earhart

text: Never interrupt someone doing what you said couldn't be done.
author: Amelia Earhart

text: Life is either a daring adventure or nothing at all.

text: Keep your face to the sunshine and you cannot see a shadow.

text: Alone we can do so little; together we can do so much.

text: The best and most beautiful things in the world cannot be seen or even touched — they must be felt with the heart.

text: Optimism is the faith that leads to achievement.

text: The best preparation for tomorrow is doing your best today.

text: Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things you didn't do than by the ones you did do.

text: Don't judge each day by the harvest you reap but by the seeds that you plant.

text: Quiet minds cannot be perplexed or frightened, but go on in fortune or misfortune at their own private pace.

text: In every walk with nature one receives far more than he seeks.

text: The mountains are calling and I must go.

text: Of all the paths you take in life, make sure a few of them are dirt.

text: Look up at the stars and not down at your feet.

text: However difficult life may seem, there is always something you can do and succeed at.

text: Intelligence is the ability to adapt to change.

text: Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.

text: We are made of star-stuff.

text: Imagination will often carry us to worlds that never were. But without it we go nowhere.

text: The cosmos is within us.
author: Carl Sagan

text: The good life is one inspired by love and guided by knowledge.

text: To be without some of the things you want is an indispensable part of happiness.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: Do not fear to be eccentric in opinion, for every opinion now accepted was once eccentric.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: The time you enjoy wasting is not wasted time.

text: A good traveler has no fixed plans, and is not intent on arriving.

text: Music in the soul can be heard by the universe.
author: Lao Tzu

text: New beginnings are often disguised as painful endings.
author: Lao Tzu

text: If you are depressed you are living in the past. If you are anxious you are living in the future. If you are at peace you are living in the present.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

text: What wisdom can you find that is greater than kindness?
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

text: The world is a book and those who do not travel read only one page.
author: Saint Augustine

text: Patience is the companion of wisdom.
author: Saint Augustine

text: There is no charm equal to tenderness of heart.
author: Jane Austen

text: I declare after all there is no enjoyment like reading!
author: Jane Austen

text: Know your own happiness.
author: Jane Austen

text: It is not in the stars to hold our destiny but in ourselves.
author: William Shakespeare

text: With mirth and laughter let old wrinkles come.
author: William Shakespeare

text: How far that little candle throws his beams! So shines a good deed in a weary world.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Our doubts are traitors, and make us lose the good we oft might win by fearing to attempt.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Give every man thy ear, but few thy voice.
author: William Shakespeare

text: The night is darkest just before the dawn. And I promise you, the dawn is coming.
author: Harvey Dent

text: Happiness depends upon ourselves.
author: Aristotle

text: Through every dark night, there's a bright day after that.
author: Tupac Shakur

text: You have to be able to accept failure to get better.

text: I've failed over and over and over again in my life. And that is why I succeed.

text: Obstacles don't have to stop you. If you run into a wall, don't turn around and give up. Figure out how to climb it.

text: Champions keep playing until they get it right.

text: Pressure is a privilege.
author: Billie Jean King

text: It's the possibility of having a dream come true that makes life interesting.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: When you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you to achieve it.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: The simple things are also the most extraordinary things, and only the wise can see them.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: One day you will wake up and there won't be any more time to do the things you've always wanted. Do it now.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: The secret of life, though, is to fall seven times and to get up eight times.
author: Paulo Coelho

text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: Curious that we spend more time congratulating people who have succeeded than encouraging people who have not.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: Everything is theoretically impossible, until it is done.
author: Robert A. Heinlein

text: A person who never made a mistake never tried anything new.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Peace cannot be kept by force; it can only be achieved by understanding.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Weak people revenge. Strong people forgive. Intelligent people ignore.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Stay hungry, stay foolish.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone else's life.
author: Steve Jobs

text: The people who are crazy enough to think they can change the world are the ones who do.

text: Innovation distinguishes between a leader and a follower.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Have the courage to follow your heart and intuition.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Everything should be made as simple as possible, but not simpler.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Perfection is achieved, not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away.

text: A goal without a plan is just a wish.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: It is only with the heart that one can see rightly; what is essential is invisible to the eye.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: All grown-ups were once children — but only few of them remember it.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: If you want to build a ship, don't drum up the men to gather wood... teach them to yearn for the vast and endless sea.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: The wisest mind has something yet to learn.
author: George Santayana

text: To be interested in the changing seasons is a happier state of mind than to be hopelessly in love with spring.
author: George Santayana

text: There is no cure for birth and death save to enjoy the interval.
author: George Santayana

text: Gratitude is not only the greatest of virtues, but the parent of all others.
author: Cicero

text: While there's life, there's hope.
author: Cicero

text: The life of the dead is placed in the memory of the living.
author: Cicero

text: A room without books is like a body without a soul.
author: Cicero

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: And those who were seen dancing were thought to be insane by those who could not hear the music.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: There is always some madness in love. But there is also always some reason in madness.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: You must have chaos within you to give birth to a dancing star.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: In the midst of movement and chaos, keep stillness inside of you.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: Every time you are tempted to react in the same old way, ask if you want to be a prisoner of the past or a pioneer of the future.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: All great changes are preceded by chaos.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: The art of being wise is the art of knowing what to overlook.
author: William James

text: The greatest weapon against stress is our ability to choose one thought over another.
author: William James

text: Begin to be now what you will be hereafter.
author: William James

text: Change your thoughts and you change your world.
author: Norman Vincent Peale

text: Shoot for the moon. Even if you miss, you'll land among the stars.
author: Norman Vincent Peale

text: Watch your manner of speech if you wish to develop a peaceful state of mind.
author: Norman Vincent Peale

text: It's not whether you get knocked down, it's whether you get up.
author: Vince Lombardi

text: Perfection is not attainable, but if we chase perfection we can catch excellence.
author: Vince Lombardi

text: The only place success comes before work is in the dictionary.
author: Vince Lombardi

text: Don't count the days, make the days count.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: He who is not courageous enough to take risks will accomplish nothing in life.

text: It isn't the mountains ahead to climb that wear you out; it's the pebble in your shoe.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: Service to others is the rent you pay for your room here on earth.

text: A man who views the world the same at fifty as he did at twenty has wasted thirty years of his life.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: You may not control all the events that happen to you, but you can decide not to be reduced by them.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Do the best you can until you know better. Then when you know better, do better.
author: Maya Angelou

text: The bird a nest, the spider a web, man friendship.
author: William Blake

text: To see a World in a Grain of Sand and a Heaven in a Wild Flower.
author: William Blake

text: No bird soars too high if he soars with his own wings.
author: William Blake

text: The glory of gardening: hands in the dirt, head in the sun, heart with nature.
author: Alfred Austin

text: A society grows great when old men plant trees whose shade they know they shall never sit in.
author: Greek proverb

text: Slow and steady wins the race.
author: Aesop

text: Little friends may prove great friends.
author: Aesop

text: Gentleness and kind persuasion win where force and bluster fail.
author: Aesop

text: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
author: Aesop

text: After the rain, the sun will reappear. After the pain, joy will still be here.
author: Walt Disney

text: The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
author: Walt Disney

text: If you can dream it, you can do it.
author: Walt Disney

text: All our dreams can come true, if we have the courage to pursue them.
author: Walt Disney

text: It's kind of fun to do the impossible.
author: Walt Disney
